features.summary: Nike reported strong Q2 FY21 results with 9% revenue growth driven by 80% digital growth and 19% growth in Greater China. The company successfully normalized inventory levels and returned to growth across all geographies. Digital now represents nearly 25% of North America business, with membership growing by 70 million new members since the pandemic began. Nike raised full-year revenue guidance to low-teens growth and expects gross margin expansion up to 50 basis points.
features.management_outlook: Management remains optimistic about long-term growth prospects despite ongoing pandemic uncertainty. They expect digital penetration to reach 50% of total business in the near future, well ahead of original 30% target. The company plans to accelerate investments in demand creation, digital transformation, and store expansion in the second half. Management emphasized they are managing for the long term and positioning Nike for accelerated growth once the pandemic subsides.
features.risks: Continued COVID-19 volatility poses ongoing operational challenges, with about 80% of EMEA stores currently open under modified hours. Supply chain bottlenecks and government restrictions across Europe and North America create uncertainty. The company faces foreign exchange headwinds of approximately 35 basis points for the full year. Wholesale channel disruption continues, particularly with undifferentiated accounts in North America declining at an accelerated pace.
features.key_drivers: Digital transformation remains the primary growth engine with 80% global digital growth for three consecutive quarters. Strong brand momentum and innovation pipeline, including successful launches like LeBron 18 and Kyrie 7, drive consumer engagement. Membership growth and engagement metrics continue accelerating, with Member Days reaching 60 million members across 25 countries. Express Lane capabilities now represent almost 20% of business, providing supply chain flexibility and responsiveness.
